title: oxalis adspersa
intro: Oxalis adspersa, also known as the spotted wood-sorrel or spotted oxalis, is a perennial plant species in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects due to the presence of oxalic acid and other compounds. Oxalis adspersa can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is believed to offer health benefits such as treating fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as acting as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent.
